documentTitle: Bundle — Initial ACO Application 2021 + Renewal Application 2024

cards.text: The person presents persistent auditory hallucinations according to the initial assessment.
The person refused voluntary hospitalization proposed in October 2021.
A diagnosis of schizoaffective disorder is made for the first time in this document.
The evaluator notes partial improvement after four weeks of treatment in a closed unit.
At the end of the 2021-2022 authorization period, the treating psychiatrist recommends a gradual return to an open setting.
cards.humanNote: Favorable recommendation not referenced in the 2024 renewal.

cards.text: The person maintains regular contact with family and her support network according to the 2022 closure report.
The prognosis at the end of the 2021-2022 period is described as 'moderately favorable with outpatient follow-up'.
No violent incident is documented during the 2021-2022 authorization period.
The person participated in a vocational reintegration program for six months in 2022.
Outpatient follow-up after the authorization was conducted twice monthly according to the care plan.
The renewal application asserts that the person poses an imminent danger without describing any incident since 2022.
The renewal cites the 2021 auditory hallucinations as evidence of the persistence of the disorder in 2024.
cards.humanNote: 2021 hallucinations are presented as if they were current symptoms.

cards.text: The schizoaffective disorder diagnosis is reformulated as paranoid schizophrenia without explanation of this nosological change.
The 2021 recommendation for a gradual return to an open setting is not mentioned in the renewal.
The renewal describes the 2022-2023 period as 'prolonged non-compliance' without specifying the underlying facts.
cards.humanNote: Contradicts outpatient follow-up notes documenting consistent compliance.

cards.text: The person's participation in the 2022 vocational reintegration program is not mentioned.
The 2021 refusal of voluntary hospitalization is presented as a recurring pattern without documentation of subsequent refusals.
The absence of violent incidents during 2021-2022 is not referenced in the renewal.
The applicant states that 'all alternatives have been tried' without reference to the outpatient follow-up that was actually carried out.
The favorable 2021 prognosis is replaced by a 'guarded' prognosis in 2024 without a new documented clinical assessment.
A 2018 report — predating the initial 2021 application — is cited as a current clinical reference.
The requested authorization period increases from 24 months (2021) to 36 months (2024) without documented justification.
The regular family contacts documented in 2022 are described in 2024 as 'conflictual and unstable'.
No neuropsychological evaluation is produced in support of the incapacity conclusion.
The applicant is a different physician than the one who followed the person during 2021-2023.
cards.humanNote: The signing physician met the person only once before filing the application.
